description: Federated driver-data sources, keyed by a short stable source id. OpenISD links to driver libraries wherever their owners maintain them, instead of copying the files. The in-app driver browser reads this map (and you can paste any GitHub repo ad hoc). A driver's unique identity is its source key + its path within that source — never its display name, which can repeat. Add a source by opening a PR that adds a keyed entry here; the key is the drivers/ folder name for bundled sources, or a short slug for federated ones.

demos.name: OpenISD demos
demos.description: Generic demo drivers shipped with OpenISD (a 6.5" woofer and a 1" tweeter) — not real products; representative values for trying the tool. The woofer is the app's default driver.

matt.name: Matt (mtg90) / AVS Forum
matt.description: ~430 drivers from the mtg90 AVS Forum collection. See drivers/matt/meta.json for provenance.

winisd-drivers.name: WinISD Drivers Archive
winisd-drivers.description: Federated driver datasheets repository containing thousands of scraped and verified speaker driver definitions.
winisd-drivers.license: public product data / factual T/S measurements

winisd.name: WinISD
winisd.description: 1121 driver files from the original WinISD distribution (circa 2006 or earlier). Quality=L (obsolete, unchecked). Most drivers are likely subsumed by more recent sources.
